enrichment.short_description: No-code automation platform offering 20M+ pre-built, one-click automations across 1,500+ apps.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Integrately offers 20M+ pre-built, one-click automations for 1,500+ apps; free plan plus paid tiers from $19.99/month.

features.title: SmartConnect
features.description: Automatically maps and connects app accounts without manual field mapping.

features.title: 1-Click automations
features.description: Activate pre-built, ready-to-use automations in three steps: select, connect, activate.

features.title: Multi-step workflows
features.description: Starter plan and above add multi-step automations with webhooks; Professional and above add branching, iterators, and auto-retry.

features.title: Premium app access
features.description: Paid plans unlock premium app connectors, from 3 apps on Starter to unlimited on Professional and above.

features.title: Free plan
features.description: A free plan offers 100 tasks per month with single-step automations and 24/5 live chat support.

pros: 20M+ pre-built automations across 1,500+ apps reduce setup time versus building flows from scratch
Free plan available with 100 tasks/month and 24/5 live chat support
Tiered pricing scales from solo users up to 150,000 tasks/month for larger teams
cons: Free and Starter plans lack branching logic, limiting complex multi-step workflows
Multiple users require the Growth plan or higher, raising cost for teams

faqs.question: Does Integrately have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free plan includes 100 tasks per month with single-step automations.

faqs.question: How many apps does Integrately support?
faqs.answer: The platform lists ready-made automations for 1,500+ applications.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for paid plans?
faqs.answer: Integrately offers a 14-day free trial on paid plans.

faqs.question: What is SmartConnect?
faqs.answer: SmartConnect is Integrately's feature that automatically connects app accounts without manual configuration.

use_cases.title: Pre-built app-to-app automation
use_cases.description: Teams use Integrately's ready-made one-click automations to connect CRM, marketing, and support apps without building flows from scratch.

use_cases.title: Multi-step workflow automation
use_cases.description: Professional and Growth plans support branching and iterators for more complex cross-app processes.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Integrately?
paragraphs: Integrately is a no-code automation platform built around a library of more than 20 million pre-built, one-click automations spanning 1,500+ applications.
Instead of building workflows step by step, users search for a ready-made automation, connect their app accounts, and activate it.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Integrately is for
paragraphs: Integrately targets small and mid-sized business teams that want common app-to-app automations, such as CRM, forms, marketing, and project management, without configuring flows manually. Its paid tiers add multi-step logic, branching, and higher task volumes for teams with more complex needs.

enrichment.short_description: No-code integration platform connecting 2,000+ apps into multi-step workflows, with triggers that don't consume task quota.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Pabbly Connect links 2,000+ apps into no-code workflows; free plan for 100 tasks/month, paid plans from about $14/month.

features.title: Workflow builder
features.description: No-code interface for creating multi-step automations across apps.

features.title: 2,000+ integrations
features.description: Connects CRM, marketing, e-commerce, and helpdesk tools.

features.title: Free internal tasks
features.description: Triggers, filters, routers, and formatters don't count against the monthly task quota.

features.title: Code steps
features.description: Built-in JavaScript and Python modules for custom logic within a workflow.

features.title: Advanced routing
features.description: Path routers, iterators, schedulers, delays, and email parsing for complex workflows.

pros: Triggers, filters, routers, and formatters are free and don't consume the monthly task quota
Free plan available (100 tasks/month) with no credit card required
30-day money-back guarantee on paid plans
cons: Free plan is limited to 2-step workflows
Lifetime/one-time pricing offers are marketed as limited-time promotions, not standard pricing

faqs.question: Is there a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pabbly Connect offers a free plan with 100 tasks per month.

faqs.question: Do triggers count toward the task limit?
faqs.answer: No, only action steps consume tasks; triggers and internal tools like filters and routers are free.

faqs.question: Does Pabbly Connect support custom code?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes JavaScript and Python modules for custom logic.

faqs.question: Is there a refund guarantee?
faqs.answer: Pabbly offers a 30-day money-back guarantee.

use_cases.title: Connecting CRM and marketing tools
use_cases.description: Businesses sync leads and customer data automatically between CRM, email marketing, and e-commerce apps.

use_cases.title: Custom logic workflows
use_cases.description: Technical teams add JavaScript or Python code steps to handle data transformations that pre-built actions can't cover.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Pabbly Connect?
paragraphs: Pabbly Connect is a no-code integration platform that links more than 2,000 applications into multi-step automated workflows.
A key differentiator is that triggers and internal helper steps (filters, routers, formatters) don't consume the task quota — only action steps do.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Pabbly Connect is for
paragraphs: It suits small businesses and technical marketers who want to automate app-to-app data flows and are price-sensitive, since triggers and internal logic steps are free and don't count toward usage limits.

enrichment.short_description: Enterprise integration platform (iPaaS) that connects business systems and also builds and governs AI agents and workflows.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Workato is an enterprise iPaaS for integrating business apps, plus tools for building and governing AI agents; pricing is quote-based.

features.title: Enterprise iPaaS
features.description: Connects business apps and data sources across the organization.

features.title: Agent Studio & orchestration
features.description: Build and manage AI agents, plus pre-built "Genies" for IT, Sales, HR, and Support functions.

features.title: API management
features.description: Governance and management tooling for enterprise APIs.

features.title: MCP gateway
features.description: Enterprise support for connecting AI models via the Model Context Protocol.

features.title: Low-code apps & RPA
features.description: Build internal apps and automate manual tasks with robotic process automation.

features.title: Master data management
features.description: Data orchestration and data hub functionality.

pros: Broad platform covering integration, API management, RPA, and AI agent orchestration in one product
Recognized as a Leader multiple times in Gartner's Magic Quadrant for iPaaS
Regional hosting available across US, Europe, Singapore, Japan, Korea, Australia, and Israel
cons: Pricing isn't published; prospects must contact sales for a quote
Enterprise focus and feature breadth may be more than smaller teams need

faqs.question: How much does Workato cost?
faqs.answer: Workato doesn't publish pricing; it describes a "Predictable Pricing Commitment" but requires contacting sales for a quote.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: A free trial is available by request through the website.

faqs.question: What is Workato used for?
faqs.answer: It's used for integrating enterprise applications and for building and governing AI agents and automated workflows.

faqs.question: What languages does the platform support?
faqs.answer: The interface supports English, Korean, and Japanese.

use_cases.title: Enterprise application integration
use_cases.description: IT and operations teams connect systems like Salesforce, NetSuite, ServiceNow, and Workday into unified workflows.

use_cases.title: AI agent orchestration
use_cases.description: Teams use Agent Studio and pre-built Genies to deploy governed AI agents for functions like sales, HR, and support.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Workato?
paragraphs: Workato is an enterprise integration platform (iPaaS) that connects business applications and data sources, and has expanded into building and governing AI agents.
It combines integration, API management, RPA, master data management, and an AI Agent Studio in one platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Workato is for
paragraphs: Workato targets enterprise IT and operations teams that need to integrate many business systems and are also exploring governed AI agent deployment across departments like IT, Sales, HR, and Support.

enrichment.short_description: Enterprise integration and AI agent orchestration platform, now branded Tray.ai, connecting 700+ business applications.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Tray.ai (formerly Tray.io) is an enterprise iPaaS for building AI agents and automations across 700+ connected apps; pricing is quote-based.

features.title: Merlin Agent Builder
features.description: No-code builder for creating AI agents with guardrails and a composable agent hub.

features.title: Agent Gateway for MCP
features.description: Managed MCP servers that expose 700+ connectors as governed tools, with audit and observability.

features.title: Intelligent iPaaS
features.description: Integration and automation across 700+ apps including Salesforce, Slack, HubSpot, and Snowflake.

features.title: AI governance
features.description: Role-based access control, audit trails, and compliance tooling for AI and automation workflows.

features.title: Dual build modes
features.description: A visual low-code builder (Tray Build) and a code-first mode for AI IDEs like Claude Code (Tray Headless).

pros: 700+ pre-built connectors across CRM, marketing, data, HR, and developer tools
SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA, and GDPR compliance noted for enterprise governance needs
Free trial offered so prospects can explore the platform before committing
cons: Pricing is quote-based across all three plans (Pro, Team, Enterprise); no published rates
Rebranded from Tray.io to Tray.ai, which can cause confusion with older references to the previous name

faqs.question: Is Tray.io still around?
faqs.answer: The company has rebranded its product and site to Tray.ai; it operates as the same platform under the new name.

faqs.question: How much does Tray.ai cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing is usage-based and quote-only; the site states pricing is "based on your usage and the features you need."

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Tray.ai offers a free trial to explore the platform.

faqs.question: What is Agent Gateway?
faqs.answer: Agent Gateway exposes Tray's 700+ connectors as governed tools through managed MCP servers.

use_cases.title: AI agent building
use_cases.description: Teams use Merlin Agent Builder to create no-code AI agents with guardrails for business processes.

use_cases.title: Enterprise app integration
use_cases.description: IT teams connect 700+ business applications, including CRM, data platforms, and HR tools, into governed automated workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Tray.io (Tray.ai)?
paragraphs: Tray.io rebranded its product and website to Tray.ai, an enterprise orchestration platform for building AI agents, governing the Model Context Protocol (MCP), and integrating 700+ applications.
It offers both a visual low-code builder and a code-first mode designed for AI IDEs.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Tray.ai is for
paragraphs: It targets enterprise IT and automation teams that need to integrate many business applications while also building and governing AI agents with audit trails and compliance controls.

enrichment.short_description: AI agent platform that turns natural-language descriptions of ops/finance processes into automated, auditable workflows.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Parabola lets ops and finance teams describe processes in plain language to build automated, auditable workflows across 1,000+ integrations.

features.title: Natural language process builder
features.description: Describe a process in plain language and Parabola drafts a workflow, asking clarifying questions along the way.

features.title: Visual, editable workflows
features.description: Each step's logic is visible and can be edited without rebuilding the workflow from scratch.

features.title: 1,000+ integrations
features.description: Connects to ERPs, data warehouses, email, and file storage systems.

features.title: Scheduled execution
features.description: Workflows run hourly, daily, or weekly, with retry logic and failure alerts.

features.title: Audit trail & governance
features.description: Role-based access, version history, and full audit logs for compliance.

features.title: Pre-built prompt templates
features.description: Ready-made templates for inventory reconciliation, invoice auditing, PO matching, and variance analysis.

pros: Free Basic plan includes 1,000 credits/month for a single user with no seat cost
Pre-built prompt templates for common ops/finance processes reduce setup time
Full audit logs and version history support compliance-sensitive workflows
cons: Business plan pricing is custom/quote-only and not published
Exact self-serve paid-tier pricing beyond the free Basic plan isn't disclosed on the pricing page

faqs.question: Is there a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Basic plan is free and includes 1,000 credits per month for one user.

faqs.question: How does Parabola build workflows?
faqs.answer: Users describe a process in natural language; Parabola asks clarifying questions and builds an editable, documented workflow.

faqs.question: How many integrations does Parabola support?
faqs.answer: The platform connects to 1,000+ systems, including ERPs, data warehouses, email, and file storage.

faqs.question: Is Business plan pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, Business plan pricing requires a sales conversation and includes a free proof of concept.

use_cases.title: Inventory & invoice reconciliation
use_cases.description: Finance teams automate inventory reconciliation, parcel invoice auditing, and three-way PO matching using pre-built prompts.

use_cases.title: Ops process automation
use_cases.description: Operations teams turn recurring manual processes described in plain language into scheduled, auditable workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Parabola?
paragraphs: Parabola is an AI agent platform for operations and finance teams: users describe a messy recurring process in natural language, and Parabola builds a transparent, editable workflow that runs automatically.
Workflows connect to 1,000+ systems and include retry logic, scheduling, and full audit trails.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Parabola is for
paragraphs: It's aimed at ops and finance teams handling recurring, rules-based work like reconciliations, invoice auditing, and PO matching, who want to automate it without writing code or managing a traditional automation tool.

enrichment.short_description: AI-powered automation tool for go-to-market teams that scrapes, researches, qualifies, and enriches leads.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Bardeen automates lead scraping, AI research, and enrichment for GTM teams; plans start at $10/month with 100 credits included.

features.title: Web scraper
features.description: Extracts data from websites to build lead lists.

features.title: Web search
features.description: AI-powered lead research and instant answers pulled from target websites.

features.title: Enrichment
features.description: Validates and appends contact information to leads.

features.title: AI qualification
features.description: Uses AI to score and prioritize leads against custom criteria.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Syncs enriched data to Google Sheets, Airtable, and Notion.

pros: Combines scraping, AI research, enrichment, and qualification in a single tool
SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, and CASA Tier 2/3 certifications for enterprise security needs
Direct sync to Google Sheets, Airtable, and Notion without manual export/import
cons: No standalone free plan; the cheapest tier (Basic) costs $10/month
Credits expire at the end of each billing period and don't roll over
Enterprise plan pricing is custom and not published

faqs.question: Does Bardeen have a free plan?
faqs.answer: No, there's no $0 tier; the Basic plan starts at $10/month, and every plan includes 100 free credits monthly.

faqs.question: What does Bardeen do?
faqs.answer: It automates lead scraping, AI research, enrichment, and qualification for go-to-market teams.

faqs.question: Where can enriched leads be sent?
faqs.answer: Bardeen syncs data to Google Sheets, Airtable, and Notion.

faqs.question: Is Bardeen SOC 2 compliant?
faqs.answer: Yes, the site states SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, and CASA Tier 2/3 certifications.

use_cases.title: Lead list building
use_cases.description: SDRs and AEs scrape and enrich lead lists from target websites without manual research.

use_cases.title: Account research automation
use_cases.description: RevOps and sales engineering teams use AI-powered web search to answer account-qualification questions at scale.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Bardeen?
paragraphs: Bardeen is an automation platform focused on go-to-market teams: it scrapes web data, runs AI-powered research and lead qualification, and enriches contact information.
Results sync directly to Google Sheets, Airtable, or Notion, and usage is metered through a monthly credit system.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Bardeen is for
paragraphs: It's built for SDRs, account executives, customer success, and RevOps teams that need to find, qualify, and enrich leads without manual research, starting at $10/month for individual use.

name: Relay.app
skip_reason: The official site now shows a shutdown notice: Relay.app is discontinuing service, shutting down August 15, 2026 for free users and September 14, 2026 for paying customers, with new signups and free-to-paid upgrades already disabled. Not appropriate to publish standard editorial content for a product actively winding down.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, code-first orchestration platform for building scripts, workflows, and internal apps in Python, TypeScript, Go, and more.
enrichment.headquarters: San Francisco, USA and Paris, France
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Windmill is an open-source, code-first platform for building workflows, scripts, and internal apps in Python, TypeScript, Go, and more languages.

features.title: Scripts
features.description: Write scripts in Python, TypeScript, Go, Bash, PHP, SQL, and 20+ other languages with auto-generated APIs and forms.

features.title: Workflows
features.description: DAG-based orchestration for representing step dependencies.

features.title: Internal apps
features.description: Low-code builder combining drag-and-drop with custom code.

features.title: Auto-generated UIs
features.description: Interfaces generated automatically from script parameters.

features.title: Scheduling & webhooks
features.description: Built-in scheduling and auto-generated webhooks for every script or flow.

features.title: Observability
features.description: Real-time logging, execution tracking, audit trails, and alerting.

pros: Fully open-source and self-hostable via Docker or Kubernetes
Free tier supports up to 50 users, 3 workspaces, and unlimited executions
Supports 20+ programming languages for scripts rather than a single proprietary language
cons: Enterprise features (SAML with group sync, priority support, advanced observability) require the paid Enterprise tier starting at $120/month
Per-worker and per-seat compute pricing on Enterprise can add up for larger teams

faqs.question: Is Windmill open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's fully open-source and can be self-hosted on Docker or Kubernetes.

faqs.question: Is there a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the free/open-source plan supports up to 50 users and unlimited executions, self-hosted or on Windmill's cloud.

faqs.question: What languages does Windmill support for scripts?
faqs.answer: Python, TypeScript, Go, Bash, PHP, SQL, and 20+ other languages.

faqs.question: How is Enterprise priced?
faqs.answer: It starts at $120/month base, plus per-developer ($20/mo), per-operator ($10/mo), and per-worker ($50/mo) compute pricing.

use_cases.title: Internal tool building
use_cases.description: Engineering teams build internal apps and admin tools with UIs auto-generated from script parameters.

use_cases.title: Data pipeline orchestration
use_cases.description: Teams orchestrate DAG-based data workflows connecting Postgres, Snowflake, Kafka, and other systems.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Windmill?
paragraphs: Windmill is an open-source, code-first orchestration platform for building scripts, DAG-based workflows, and internal applications from a single interface.
Scripts can be written in Python, TypeScript, Go, Bash, PHP, SQL, and 20+ other languages, with Windmill auto-generating APIs, forms, and webhooks for each one.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Windmill is for
paragraphs: It's aimed at engineering teams that want a code-first alternative to visual-only automation tools, with the option to self-host for free or use the paid Enterprise tier for SSO, audit logs, and priority support.

enrichment.short_description: Python-native workflow orchestration platform that adds durable execution, retries, and observability via a @flow decorator.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Prefect turns Python functions into durable, observable workflows with retries and scheduling; a free Hobby tier and paid Cloud plans exist.

features.title: @flow decorator
features.description: Turns existing Python functions into orchestrated workflows without a new framework.

features.title: Durable execution
features.description: Built-in retry logic, state tracking, and automatic crash recovery.

features.title: Flexible deployment
features.description: Run on Prefect Cloud, your own VPC, Kubernetes, ECS, or serverless.

features.title: Observability dashboard
features.description: Unified view of runs, logs, and states across teams.

features.title: Scheduling & automation
features.description: Cron and event-driven triggers, automatic backfills, and SLA monitoring.

features.title: Open-source core
features.description: The Prefect framework is open source (Apache 2.0) with 40,000+ GitHub stars.

pros: Free open-source framework plus a free Hobby tier on Prefect Cloud
Python-native — adds orchestration via a decorator rather than a new DSL
40,000+ GitHub stars indicate a large open-source community
cons: Detailed pricing for the Starter, Team, and Pro Cloud tiers isn't published
Hobby tier is capped at 2 users, 1 workspace, and 5 deployments

faqs.question: Is Prefect free?
faqs.answer: The core Prefect framework is open source, and Prefect Cloud has a free Hobby tier (2 users, 1 workspace, 5 deployments, 500 credits/month).

faqs.question: What language does Prefect use?
faqs.answer: Prefect workflows are written in Python.

faqs.question: Did Prefect acquire Dagster Labs?
faqs.answer: The Prefect site states that Prefect acquired Dagster Labs in July 2026.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for paid plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free trial for paid Cloud plans is mentioned in the FAQ.

use_cases.title: ML and data pipeline orchestration
use_cases.description: Data and ML teams convert existing Python functions into scheduled, retryable, observable pipelines.

use_cases.title: Agent workflow orchestration
use_cases.description: Teams use Prefect to add durable execution and observability to AI agent workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Prefect?
paragraphs: Prefect is a Python-native workflow orchestration platform: adding a @flow decorator to existing functions gives them retries, state tracking, scheduling, and crash recovery without a new DSL.
It can run on managed Prefect Cloud or within your own infrastructure (VPC, Kubernetes, ECS, or serverless), and the core framework is open source.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Prefect is for
paragraphs: It's aimed at data engineering and ML teams already working in Python who want durable, observable orchestration without rewriting pipelines in a new framework. In July 2026, Prefect announced it had acquired Dagster Labs.

enrichment.short_description: AI-native data orchestration platform (Dagster+) built on the open-source Dagster framework, with asset-based pipelines.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Dagster+ is a hosted data orchestration platform on the open-source Dagster framework; Solo plans start at $120/month with a 30-day trial.

features.title: Asset-based orchestration
features.description: Model data pipelines as assets across your entire technology stack.

features.title: Built-in observability
features.description: Lineage, dependency tracking, and data health monitoring.

features.title: Dagster+ AI
features.description: AI-assisted diagnostics and automated remediation for pipeline issues.

features.title: Branch deployments
features.description: Test pipeline changes safely before merging to production.

features.title: Hybrid deployment
features.description: Run in the cloud, on-premises, or a mix of both.

features.title: First-class integrations
features.description: Native support for dbt, Snowflake, and Fivetran.

pros: Core orchestration framework is open source and free to self-host
Native integrations with dbt, Snowflake, and Fivetran cover common modern data stack tools
Branch deployments let teams test pipeline changes before they reach production
cons: Dagster+ (the hosted product) has no permanent free tier — only a 30-day free trial on Solo and Starter plans
Starter plan jumps to $1,200/month (effective May 2026), a steep step up from the $120/month Solo plan
Prefect announced it acquired Dagster Labs in July 2026, which could affect the product's future roadmap

faqs.question: Is Dagster free?
faqs.answer: The open-source Dagster orchestration framework is free and self-hostable; the hosted Dagster+ product starts at $120/month after a 30-day free trial.

faqs.question: Was Dagster acquired?
faqs.answer: Yes — Prefect's site states that Prefect acquired Dagster Labs, the company behind Dagster, in July 2026.

faqs.question: What data tools does Dagster integrate with?
faqs.answer: It lists first-class integrations with dbt, Snowflake, and Fivetran.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for Dagster+?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 30-day free trial is available for the Solo and Starter plans.

use_cases.title: Data pipeline orchestration
use_cases.description: Data engineering teams model and orchestrate pipelines as assets with built-in lineage and observability.

use_cases.title: Data quality & cost monitoring
use_cases.description: Teams use Dagster+'s catalog, quality monitoring, and cost insights to keep data pipelines healthy and within budget.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Dagster?
paragraphs: Dagster is a data orchestration platform built around an open-source, asset-based framework, with a hosted commercial product (Dagster+) adding observability, a data catalog, and AI-assisted diagnostics.
It integrates natively with dbt, Snowflake, and Fivetran, and supports hybrid deployment across cloud and on-premises infrastructure.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Dagster is for
paragraphs: It's built for data engineering teams that want asset-based pipeline orchestration with strong observability, either by self-hosting the open-source framework for free or paying for the hosted Dagster+ platform starting at $120/month.
